content.problemStatement:
in every century, there is a chosen one. Therefore, we do not live in a society
--Last paragraphs of the IZhO 2022 Editorial

K0Kalaru47 was out with his friends and he started to tell them about a CP Task. Because the statement was informal, his friends quickly parried with "Who Asked", so here is the formal statement:

Given arrays PPP and CCC of size (N+K)(N+K)(N+K) each, the beauty of an array BBB of size (N+K)(N+K)(N+K) is defined as the sum of CiC_iCi‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§(N+K)1\leq i \leq (N+K)1‚Č§i‚Č§(N+K) such that Bi=PiB_i = P_iBi‚Äč=Pi‚Äč.

More formally, the beauty of an array BBB is equal to ‚ąĎi=1‚ą£B‚ą£Ci\large\sum^{|B|}_{i=1} C_i‚ąĎi=1‚ą£B‚ą£‚ÄčCi‚Äč [Bi=Pi][B_i = P_i][Bi‚Äč=Pi‚Äč]

Given an array AAA of size NNN such that (1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M)(1 \leq A_i \leq M)(1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M).
Your task is to insert KKK elements into the array such that:


Each inserted element lies in the range [1,M][1, M][1,M].
The beauty of the final array is **maximized**.
Note that, an element can be inserted at any index of the array.

You should find two values:


The **maximum** beauty that can be obtained across all arrays of size (N+K)(N+K)(N+K).
The **number of distinct arrays** of size (N+K)(N+K)(N+K) having the maximum beauty. Note that, two arrays XXX and YYY are said to be different if there exists some index iii such that Xi‚Č†YiX_i ‚Č† Y_iXi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Yi‚Äč. Since the number of distinct arrays can be huge, print the value modulo MODMODMOD.

Input Format

The first line of input consists of 444 integers N,K,MN,K,MN,K,M and MODMODMOD
The second line of input contains NNN space-separated integers - the array AAA.
The third line of input contains (N+K)(N+K)(N+K) space-separated integers - the array PPP.
The fourth line of input contains (N+K)(N+K)(N+K) space-separated integers - the array CCC.


Output Format
Print two integers separated by a single space.



The first integer corresponds to the **maximum** beauty that can be obtained across all arrays of size (N+K)(N+K)(N+K).
The second integer corresponds to the **number of distinct arrays** of size (N+K)(N+K)(N+K) having the maximum beauty. Since the number of distinct arrays can be huge, print the value modulo MODMODMOD.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
By inserting one element, we can form the following distinct arrays :

B=[1,1,1]B = [1,1,1]B=[1,1,1] : The indices where Bi=PiB_i = P_iBi‚Äč=Pi‚Äč are {1,3}\{1,3\}{1,3}.
Thus, the beauty is C1+C3=1+2=3C_1+C_3 = 1+2 = 3C1‚Äč+C3‚Äč=1+2=3.
B=[1,1,2]B = [1,1,2]B=[1,1,2] : The indices where Bi=PiB_i = P_iBi‚Äč=Pi‚Äč are {1}\{1\}{1}.
Thus, the beauty is C1=1C_1 = 1C1‚Äč=1.
B=[1,2,1]B = [1,2,1]B=[1,2,1] : The indices where Bi=PiB_i = P_iBi‚Äč=Pi‚Äč are {1,2,3}\{1,2,3\}{1,2,3}.
Thus, the beauty is C1+C2+C3=1+1+2=4C_1+C_2+C_3 = 1+1+2 = 4C1‚Äč+C2‚Äč+C3‚Äč=1+1+2=4.
B=[2,1,1]B = [2,1,1]B=[2,1,1] : The indices where Bi=PiB_i = P_iBi‚Äč=Pi‚Äč are {3}\{3\}{3}.
Thus, the beauty is C3=2C_3 = 2C3‚Äč=2.

Thus, the maximum beauty possible is 444, and the number of arrays with beauty 444 is 111 which is [1,2,1][1,2,1][1,2,1].
